assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can complete CockroachDB Cloud registration.
The developer does not yet have a CockroachDB Cloud organization or cluster.
The developer can use a terminal, Git, and a language runtime required by the chosen sample.

developer_goal: Create a CockroachDB Cloud account, deploy a Standard trial cluster, and run the official quickstart sample until first queries succeed.

first_success.outcome: A Standard trial cluster and SQL user exist, and the selected language sample connects, writes, reads, and prints success.
first_success.observable_signal: For the Java sample, the terminal prints Hello world! and BUILD SUCCESSFUL after ./gradlew run.

route_selection.surface: CockroachDB Cloud console plus official Quickstart sample
route_selection.rule: Use the Cloud Quickstart self-service path that deploys Standard with trial credits, then select one fully specified sample language only after registration method is fixed.

candidates.name: Email registration then Java sample
candidates.reason: Email signup fields and post-verify name, company, and survey steps are documented, but GitHub, Google, and Microsoft SSO are peer registration methods with different external gates.

candidates.name: SSO registration then Java sample
candidates.reason: Docs present GitHub, Google, and Microsoft as peer SSO options and recommend SSO for security; the three providers are equal consequential branches.

candidates.name: Any registration then Node.js sample
candidates.reason: Node.js is a documented peer language tab in the same quickstart and reaches the same first-query outcome with different local commands.

candidates.name: Basic cluster or self-hosted local cluster
candidates.reason: The general Quickstart currently selects Standard with sample code; Basic and self-hosted are separate guides.

required_path.action: Register a CockroachDB Cloud account using email or a supported SSO provider.

required_fields.label: Authentication method
required_fields.notes: Email versus GitHub, Google, or Microsoft SSO remains unresolved.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy acceptance
Name and company details
required_fields.notes: Documented after email verification on the email route; SSO post-auth profile fields are not fully enumerated.

required_fields.label: Onboarding survey answers
required_fields.notes: Email route Finish Setup survey; whether SSO skips or alters this is unverified.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can sign in to CockroachDB Cloud and reach Get Started or Clusters.

required_path.action: If using email signup, open the verification email and select Verify my email.
required_path.observable_result: Registration continues to name, company, and survey steps on the email route.

required_path.action: Create a Standard cluster with cloud provider and region, then create a SQL user and save the generated password.
required_path.observable_result: The cluster exists and the Connect to cluster dialog provides a sample connection string.

required_path.action: Export the connection environment variable, clone the quickstart samples, and run the selected language sample.

required_fields.label: Sample language
required_fields.notes: Java and Node.js are peer tabs.

required_fields.label: ./gradlew run or npm install and npm start equivalent

required_path.observable_result: The sample prints the documented success output for the chosen language.

prerequisites.description: Git and a terminal are required to clone and run the official sample repository.
The Java sample implies a local Java toolchain usable by the Gradle wrapper; the exact version is not stated in the quickstart.

external_gates.description: Registration requires acceptance of Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
SSO registration requires authorizing CockroachDB Cloud at the identity provider.
Cluster creation without an active free trial may require adding a payment method.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email verification must arrive before email registration can finish.
Cluster creation takes a vendor-stated few seconds before the SQL-user dialog.

platform_outcomes.description: First organizations receive free trial credits usable for the Standard quickstart cluster.
CockroachDB Cloud provisions the cluster and generates the SQL user password once.

excluded.reason: Listed as alternative ways to get started after the Cloud sample path.
Optional beyond the single-region quickstart path.

sources.title: Quickstart with CockroachDB
evidence_supported: Payment note when no active trial

uncertainties.question: Which registration method is the selected shortest CockroachDB Cloud account route among email, GitHub, Google, and Microsoft?
uncertainties.impact: Auth choice changes required fields, external authorization, verification waits, and whether name, company, and survey steps appear.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh-account walkthroughs for email and each SSO provider with screenshots of every required field and post-auth screen.

uncertainties.question: Is Java or Node.js the selected sample language when both tabs reach the same first-query outcome?
uncertainties.impact: Language choice changes local prerequisites, commands, and action count after cluster creation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Apply the lexicographic route rule explicitly once registration is fixed, or obtain a vendor statement that one sample is the default measured path.

uncertainties.question: What billing screen appears if the new organization has no active free trial credits?
uncertainties.impact: A payment gate may be required before Create cluster and would change external_gates and possibly route eligibility.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official documentation or a fresh-account capture of the no-trial Finalize page.
